tasks.title: Implement usePanelResize hook
tasks.description: Create frontend/src/hooks/usePanelResize.ts. The hook accepts storageKey, defaultWidth, minWidth, maxWidth. Reads initial width from localStorage via useLocalStorage. Attaches/removes window mousemove+mouseup listeners during drag. Clamps width on every render. Returns { width, handleMouseDown }.
acceptance_criteria: Hook file exists at frontend/src/hooks/usePanelResize.ts
Returns clamped width between minWidth and maxWidth on first render when stored value is out of bounds
handleMouseDown triggers a drag sequence: mousemove updates width, mouseup removes listeners
Width is persisted to localStorage under the given storageKey after each mousemove
useEffect cleanup removes window listeners if component unmounts during an active drag
No Zustand store imports

tasks.title: Apply usePanelResize to ConfigPanel
tasks.description: In frontend/src/components/config/ConfigPanel.tsx: import usePanelResize, call it with storageKey='prism:panel-width:config', defaultWidth=480, minWidth=320, maxWidth=800. Replace w-[480px] on <aside> with style={{ width }}. Add relative to <aside> className. Render drag handle as first child of <aside>.
acceptance_criteria: w-[480px] class is removed from ConfigPanel's <aside>
<aside> has position: relative (via relative class)
<aside> style attribute contains width in pixels driven by usePanelResize
Drag handle div is the first child of <aside>, has role=separator, aria-orientation=vertical, cursor-col-resize
Dragging the handle left/right changes the panel width visually
Width persists across page reload under localStorage key prism:panel-width:config
Existing ConfigPanel tests pass

tasks.title: Apply usePanelResize to AgentSettingsPanel
tasks.description: In frontend/src/components/agent-launcher/AgentSettingsPanel.tsx: identical changes as T-002 but with storageKey='prism:panel-width:agent-settings'.
acceptance_criteria: w-[480px] class is removed from AgentSettingsPanel's <aside>
<aside> has position: relative (via relative class)
<aside> style attribute contains width in pixels driven by usePanelResize
Drag handle div is the first child of <aside>
Dragging the handle changes the panel width visually
Width persists across page reload under localStorage key prism:panel-width:agent-settings
Existing AgentSettingsPanel tests pass

tasks.title: Unit tests for usePanelResize
tasks.description: Create frontend/__tests__/hooks/usePanelResize.test.ts. Test: initial width read from localStorage, clamping of out-of-bound stored values, drag sequence (mousedown → mousemove → mouseup), listener cleanup on unmount during drag, localStorage write after each move.
acceptance_criteria: Test file exists at frontend/__tests__/hooks/usePanelResize.test.ts
Test: hook returns defaultWidth when localStorage is empty
Test: hook returns clamped minWidth when stored value is below minimum
Test: hook returns clamped maxWidth when stored value is above maximum
Test: handleMouseDown + simulated mousemove updates width and writes to localStorage
Test: mouseup removes window event listeners
Test: unmounting during an active drag does not throw and removes listeners
All tests pass with vitest

tasks.title: Update ConfigPanel and AgentSettingsPanel render tests
tasks.description: Update frontend/__tests__/components/ConfigPanel... and AgentSettingsPanel... tests to assert presence of drag handle (role=separator) and absence of w-[480px] class on <aside>. Existing tests must be updated rather than deleted.
acceptance_criteria: ConfigPanel test asserts getByRole('separator') exists
AgentSettingsPanel test asserts getByRole('separator') exists
No test asserts presence of w-[480px] on <aside>
All existing assertions that are unrelated to sizing continue to pass
